content: read reviews/1042.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/1042.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/1042.md.

# Review: deepmind/1042.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 1042`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/1042`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (connected components of sublevel sets of monic polynomials with roots in a closed set of prescribed transfinite diameter).
- [x] Bibliography entries present: `[EHP58]` (original Erdős–Herzog–Piranian paper) and `[GhRa24]` (Ghosh–Ramachandran, who solved both parts).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos1042 ... end Erdos1042`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos1042`).
- [x] `open` statements appear **before** the namespace block: `open Classical Filter Finset`.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `pairwiseDistProd` has a LaTeX docstring describing the product of pairwise distances.
- [x] `nthTransfiniteDiam` has a LaTeX docstring with the formula for $d_n(F)$.
- [x] `transfiniteDiameter` has a LaTeX docstring identifying it as the logarithmic capacity.
- [x] `sublevelSet` has a docstring describing the sublevel set of a monic polynomial.
- [x] `pairwiseDistProd`, `nthTransfiniteDiam`, and `transfiniteDiameter` are marked `noncomputable` (they use `sSup`, `lim`, and real-valued operations via classical choice).
- [x] `sublevelSet` is `def` (not `noncomputable`) — correct since it defines a `Set ℂ` (a predicate), which does not require noncomputability.
- [x] No `sorry` in any definition.
- [x] All definition names use `lowerCamelCase`: `pairwiseDistProd`, `nthTransfiniteDiam`, `transfiniteDiameter`, `sublevelSet`.
- [x] No local notation used (none needed).
- [x] No `@[category test]` / `@[category API]` sanity checks (optional — acceptable to omit).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Both declarations use the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_1042` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
- [x] Names follow Lean/Mathlib conventions: `erdos_1042`, `erdos_1042.variants.upper_bound`.
- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 30 31]`.
- [x] Both theorems have docstrings referencing `[GhRa24]`.

### `answer()` usage

The original problem is a two-part assertion: (1) existence of a closed set with certain properties, and (2) an upper bound on connected components. Both are **Case 3 — direct assertions** (no yes/no question, no "find the value"). Accordingly:

- [x] No `answer()` wrapper is used — correct for Case 3.
- [x] The variant `erdos_1042.variants.upper_bound` takes `(F : Set ℂ)` and hypothesis parameters. This is acceptable: the "no quantifiers as parameters" rule applies only to `answer()`-bearing theorems.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has **exactly one** `@[category ...]`.
- [x] Category value is `research solved` — valid, consistent with `[GhRa24]` having solved both parts.
- [x] N/A: not `research open`, so the sorry-free-proof check does not apply.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has **exactly one** `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed: `30 31`.
- [x] Numbers are in ascending numeric order: 30 < 31.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both 30 (Functions of a complex variable) and 31 (Potential theory) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Both theorems use `by` + indented `sorry` (multi-line format — acceptable per checklist).
- [x] No `sorry` in any `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declaration.
- [x] No informal proof sketches included.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [x] Variant is named `erdos_1042.variants.upper_bound` (correct naming convention).
- [x] Variant lives in the **same file** as the main theorem.
- [x] Variant has its own `@[category research solved, AMS 30 31]` attributes.
- [x] Variant has a docstring explaining it formalizes the upper-bound part of the problem.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable: `ℂ`, `ℝ`, `ℕ`, `∃`, `∀`, `∈`, `⊆`, `¬`, `↥`, `→`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` — not verified in this review.
- [ ] All five custom linters pass — not verified in this review.

## Summary

**Result: PASS** (modulo build verification)

All checklist items are satisfied. The file cleanly formalizes both parts of Erdős Problem 1042 (existence and upper bound) as solved research problems, with correct `answer()` handling (Case 3 — no wrapper needed), proper AMS codes for complex/potential theory, and well-documented custom definitions for transfinite diameter and sublevel sets.

    15→-/
    16→
    17→import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18→
    19→/-!
    20→# Erdős Problem 1042
    21→
    22→*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1042](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1042)
    23→
    24→A problem of Erdős, Herzog, and Piranian on the number of connected components
    25→of sublevel sets of monic polynomials with roots in a closed set of prescribed
    26→transfinite diameter.
    27→
    28→[EHP58] Erdős, P., Herzog, F., and Piranian, G., _Metric properties of
    29→polynomials_, J. Analyse Math. 6 (1958), 125-148.
    30→
    31→[GhRa24] Ghosh, S. and Ramachandran, D., solved both parts of this problem.
    32→-/
    33→
    34→open Classical Filter Finset
    35→
    36→namespace Erdos1042
    37→
    38→/-- The product of pairwise distances $\prod_{i<j} \|z_i - z_j\|$ for a tuple of
    39→complex numbers. -/
    40→noncomputable def pairwiseDistProd {n : ℕ} (z : Fin n → ℂ) : ℝ :=
    41→  ((univ : Finset (Fin n × Fin n)).filter (fun p => p.1 < p.2)).prod
    42→    (fun p => ‖z p.1 - z p.2‖)
    43→
    44→/-- The $n$-th transfinite diameter of $F \subseteq \mathbb{C}$:
    45→$$d_n(F) = \sup_{z_1,\ldots,z_n \in F} \left(\prod_{i<j} |z_i - z_j|\right)^{2/(n(n-1))}.$$ -/
    46→noncomputable def nthTransfiniteDiam (F : Set ℂ) (n : ℕ) : ℝ :=
    47→  sSup {t : ℝ | ∃ z : Fin n → ℂ, (∀ i, z i ∈ F) ∧
    48→    t = (pairwiseDistProd z) ^ ((2 : ℝ) / (↑n * (↑n - 1)))}
    49→
    50→/-- The transfinite diameter (logarithmic capacity) of $F \subseteq \mathbb{C}$:
    51→$$\rho(F) = \lim_{n \to \infty} d_n(F).$$ -/
    52→noncomputable def transfiniteDiameter (F : Set ℂ) : ℝ :=
    53→  lim (atTop.map (fun n => nthTransfiniteDiam F n))
    54→
    55→/-- The sublevel set $\{z : \|\prod_i(z - z_i)\| < 1\}$ of a monic polynomial with
    56→given roots. -/
    57→def sublevelSet {n : ℕ} (z : Fin n → ℂ) : Set ℂ :=
    58→  {w : ℂ | ‖(univ : Finset (Fin n)).prod (fun i => w - z i)‖ < 1}
    59→
    60→/--
    61→Erdős Problem 1042, existence [GhRa24]:
    62→
    63→There exists a closed set $F \subset \mathbb{C}$ with transfinite diameter $1$, not contained in
    64→any closed disc of radius $1$, and for infinitely many $n$ there exist $z_1,\ldots,z_n \in F$
    65→such that $\{z : |\prod(z - z_i)| < 1\}$ has exactly $n$ connected components.
    66→-/
    67→@[category research solved, AMS 30 31]
    68→theorem erdos_1042 :
    69→    ∃ (F : Set ℂ), IsClosed F ∧ transfiniteDiameter F = 1 ∧
    70→      (¬∃ c : ℂ, F ⊆ Metric.closedBall c 1) ∧
    71→      Set.Infinite {n : ℕ | ∃ z : Fin n → ℂ, (∀ i, z i ∈ F) ∧
    72→        Nat.card (ConnectedComponents ↥(sublevelSet z)) = n} := by
    73→  sorry
    74→
    75→/--
    76→Erdős Problem 1042, upper bound [GhRa24]:
    77→
    78→If $F \subset \mathbb{C}$ is closed with $0 < \text{transfinite diameter} < 1$, then there exists
    79→$c > 0$ (depending on $F$) such that for all $n$ and all $z_1,\ldots,z_n \in F$, the number of
    80→connected components of $\{z : |\prod(z - z_i)| < 1\}$ is at most $(1-c) \cdot n$.
    81→-/
    82→@[category research solved, AMS 30 31]
    83→theorem erdos_1042.variants.upper_bound (F : Set ℂ) (hF : IsClosed F)
    84→    (hd_pos : transfiniteDiameter F > 0) (hd_lt : transfiniteDiameter F < 1) :
    85→    ∃ c : ℝ, c > 0 ∧ ∀ (n : ℕ) (z : Fin n → ℂ), (∀ i, z i ∈ F) →
    86→      (Nat.card (ConnectedComponents ↥(sublevelSet z)) : ℝ) ≤ (1 - c) * n := by
    87→  sorry
    88→
    89→end Erdos1042
    90→

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erdős Problem 1042

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1042](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1042)

A problem of Erdős, Herzog, and Piranian on the number of connected components
of sublevel sets of monic polynomials with roots in a closed set of prescribed
transfinite diameter.

[EHP58] Erdős, P., Herzog, F., and Piranian, G., _Metric properties of
polynomials_, J. Analyse Math. 6 (1958), 125-148.

[GhRa24] Ghosh, S. and Ramachandran, D., solved both parts of this problem.
-/

open Classical Filter Finset

namespace Erdos1042

/-- The product of pairwise distances $\prod_{i<j} \|z_i - z_j\|$ for a tuple of
complex numbers. -/
noncomputable def pairwiseDistProd {n : ℕ} (z : Fin n → ℂ) : ℝ :=
  ((univ : Finset (Fin n × Fin n)).filter (fun p => p.1 < p.2)).prod
    (fun p => ‖z p.1 - z p.2‖)

/-- The $n$-th transfinite diameter of $F \subseteq \mathbb{C}$:
$$d_n(F) = \sup_{z_1,\ldots,z_n \in F} \left(\prod_{i<j} |z_i - z_j|\right)^{2/(n(n-1))}.$$ -/
noncomputable def nthTransfiniteDiam (F : Set ℂ) (n : ℕ) : ℝ :=
  sSup {t : ℝ | ∃ z : Fin n → ℂ, (∀ i, z i ∈ F) ∧
    t = (pairwiseDistProd z) ^ ((2 : ℝ) / (↑n * (↑n - 1)))}

/-- The transfinite diameter (logarithmic capacity) of $F \subseteq \mathbb{C}$:
$$\rho(F) = \lim_{n \to \infty} d_n(F).$$ -/
noncomputable def transfiniteDiameter (F : Set ℂ) : ℝ :=
  lim (atTop.map (fun n => nthTransfiniteDiam F n))

/-- The sublevel set $\{z : \|\prod_i(z - z_i)\| < 1\}$ of a monic polynomial with
given roots. -/
def sublevelSet {n : ℕ} (z : Fin n → ℂ) : Set ℂ :=
  {w : ℂ | ‖(univ : Finset (Fin n)).prod (fun i => w - z i)‖ < 1}

/--
Erdős Problem 1042, existence [GhRa24]:

There exists a closed set $F \subset \mathbb{C}$ with transfinite diameter $1$, not contained in
any closed disc of radius $1$, and for infinitely many $n$ there exist $z_1,\ldots,z_n \in F$
such that $\{z : |\prod(z - z_i)| < 1\}$ has exactly $n$ connected components.
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 30 31]
theorem erdos_1042 :
    ∃ (F : Set ℂ), IsClosed F ∧ transfiniteDiameter F = 1 ∧
      (¬∃ c : ℂ, F ⊆ Metric.closedBall c 1) ∧
      Set.Infinite {n : ℕ | ∃ z : Fin n → ℂ, (∀ i, z i ∈ F) ∧
        Nat.card (ConnectedComponents ↥(sublevelSet z)) = n} := by
  sorry

/--
Erdős Problem 1042, upper bound [GhRa24]:

If $F \subset \mathbb{C}$ is closed with $0 < \text{transfinite diameter} < 1$, then there exists
$c > 0$ (depending on $F$) such that for all $n$ and all $z_1,\ldots,z_n \in F$, the number of
connected components of $\{z : |\prod(z - z_i)| < 1\}$ is at most $(1-c) \cdot n$.
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 30 31]
theorem erdos_1042.variants.upper_bound (F : Set ℂ) (hF : IsClosed F)
    (hd_pos : transfiniteDiameter F > 0) (hd_lt : transfiniteDiameter F < 1) :
    ∃ c : ℝ, c > 0 ∧ ∀ (n : ℕ) (z : Fin n → ℂ), (∀ i, z i ∈ F) →
      (Nat.card (ConnectedComponents ↥(sublevelSet z)) : ℝ) ≤ (1 - c) * n := by
  sorry

end Erdos1042

content.text: The review summary says **PASS**. No changes needed.
